serving.natural_language_portion: One large black pepper crab, typically served as a main dish for sharing among 2-3 people.

nutrient_source.title: Crab, blue, cooked, moist heat (FDC ID: 172033) adapted for Black Pepper Crab sauce
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient data for cooked crab adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. Values for energy, fat, saturated fat, carbohydrate, sugar, dietary fibre, and sodium are estimated to account for the black pepper sauce components (oil, sugar, soy sauce, oyster sauce, garlic, ginger).

gi_source.title: Analogous estimate based on cooked crab and high-GI sauce ingredients
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (conceptual basis)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index is estimated based on the low GI of crab meat and the medium-to-high GI of sauce components (sugar, cornstarch). No direct GI entry for Black Pepper Crab found in the University of Sydney GI Database.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume black pepper crab in moderation due to its high sodium content (~1140 mg per 100 g), which can significantly contribute to elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugars and refined carbohydrates in sauce
type2_diabetes.guidance: For individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, portion control is important due to the added sugars and carbohydrates in the black pepper sauce, which can impact postprandial blood glucose levels.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium and moderate saturated fat content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Those with cardiovascular disease should be mindful of the high sodium and moderate saturated fat in black pepper crab, as excessive intake can negatively affect blood pressure and lipid profiles.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Black Pepper Crab is a quintessential Singaporean seafood dish featuring whole crabs stir-fried in a rich, savory, and intensely spicy black pepper sauce. The dish is characterized by its bold, peppery flavor profile, often complemented by garlic, ginger, butter, and a touch of sweetness from sugar. The crabs are typically cooked until the shell turns vibrant red, and the meat is tender and succulent. The thick, glossy black pepper sauce coats the crab pieces, making it a messy but highly enjoyable communal meal. Nutritionally, it is a good source of lean protein from the crab meat, but the sauce contributes significant amounts of sodium, fat, and added sugars. It is a celebratory dish commonly found in seafood restaurants across Singapore and is considered one of the national dishes alongside chilli crab. Clinically, the high sodium content is a concern for hypertensive individuals, while the added sugars in the sauce require portion awareness for those managing Type 2 Diabetes.

confidence_reason: Nutrient data is adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for cooked crab and estimated for the black pepper sauce components. Glycemic index is an analogous estimate based on sauce ingredients as no direct GI entry exists for this dish.

Iteration 2: Ensured consistency between flat aliases and aliases_by_language. Added missing Tamil alias 'காளி மிர்ச் கேக்ரா' to flat aliases list to resolve q8 failure.

Iteration 3: Performed systematic cross-check of all aliases_by_language entries against flat aliases. Confirmed every string in aliases_by_language['english'], aliases_by_language['chinese'], aliases_by_language['malay'], aliases_by_language['tamil'], aliases_by_language['hindi'], and aliases_by_language['romanized'] appears verbatim in flat aliases array.
